This recognition means a lot because it reflects the incredible community and partners who believe in independent media and the work we’re doing every day.

Thank you to everyone who supports MeidasTouch and helps make moments like this possible. We’re just getting started. 🎙️🏆
BIG NEWS: The MeidasTouch Network is honored to have won the Advertiser’s Choice Award at the Independent Podcast & Creator Awards during South by Southwest (SXSW). 1/
Trump on oil in Middle East: We didn’t need them before we started “dig we must.” Dig me wust is the Trump policy.

Trump started this reckless war, alienated allies for years, bragged he didn’t need anyone’s help—and now that the situation is spiraling, he’s begging the same countries he insulted to come rescue him.

And they’re saying no.

Italy says diplomacy is the answer. Greece says it won’t participate in military operations. The European Union is discussing its existing naval mission but is not expected to expand it to the Strait.

Across Europe, the response has been even colder.
Germany’s defense minister Boris Pistorius delivered perhaps the most blunt response: “What does Trump expect from a handful of European frigates that the powerful U.S. Navy cannot do? This is not our war, we have not started it.”

Even Britain, one of America’s closest allies, is distancing itself from Trump’s war. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he would not be “drawn into the wider Iran war” while discussing alternative approaches to shipping security.
South Korea signaled no commitment either, saying: "We will communicate closely with the U.S. regarding this matter and make a decision after careful review.”

Australia also made it clear they’re staying out: “We won't be sending a ship to the Strait of Hormuz. We know how incredibly important that is, but that's not something that we've been asked or that we're contributing to,” said minister Catherine King.

Japan’s Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi shut the idea down directly: “We have not made any decisions whatsoever about dispatching escort ships. We are continuing to examine what Japan can do independently and what can be done within the legal framework.”

World leaders to Trump after he begged them for help with the Strait of Hormuz: You started this mess. Now deal with it.

We've compiled a list of statements from world leaders in the aftermath of Trump's desperate posts and comments. Follow along with this thread to read them all. 🧵
He already said repeatedly he didn't need their help. And he's lying about the second part. These problems are all of Trump's making. We are witnessing karma in real time.

Trump: We strongly encourage other nations to get involved with us and get involved quickly and with great enthusiasm. I have that from a number of them and I would like to say their names but frankly I don't know if they would want me to or not because they might not want to be targeted.
